pii_awareness.title: Handling Personally Identifiable Information (PII)
pii_awareness.description: Learn how to recognize and avoid sharing PII in AI prompts.

questions.text: Which of the following should NEVER be included in an AI prompt?
options: A) A customer's full Social Security Number
B) A generic product description
C) A company's public mission statement
D) An anonymized data trend

questions.text: What should you do before pasting customer data into an AI tool?
options: A) Nothing — AI tools are always secure
B) Remove or redact all identifying information
C) Ask the customer for permission in the prompt
D) Use a VPN

questions.text: A colleague asks you to summarize a client file using AI. The file has names and phone numbers. What do you do?
options: A) Paste it in — AI won't store it
B) Redact names and numbers first, then summarize
C) Only paste the phone numbers
D) Ask the AI to delete the data after

ip_protection.title: Protecting Intellectual Property & Trade Secrets
ip_protection.description: Understand the risks of sharing proprietary information with AI tools.

questions.text: Which of these is considered a trade secret?
options: A) A press release published on your website
B) An internal pricing algorithm not shared publicly
C) Your company's public stock price
D) An open-source library your team uses

questions.text: You want AI to review your team's proprietary source code. What's the safest approach?
options: A) Paste the full codebase for best results
B) Use only non-proprietary snippets or pseudocode
C) It's fine if you delete the chat after
D) Use a personal account instead of work

general_awareness.title: General AI Safety Awareness
general_awareness.description: Foundational module covering safe and responsible use of AI tools at work.

questions.text: What is the primary risk of entering sensitive data into an AI chatbot?
options: A) The AI might give a wrong answer
B) The data could be stored, leaked, or used for training
C) The AI will send it to your manager
D) There is no risk

questions.text: Your company has an AI usage policy. When should you review it?
options: A) Only during onboarding
B) Whenever it's updated and before using a new AI tool
C) Never — policies are just guidelines
D) Only if you get flagged
